canonical_title: Apparent customer obstacles can function as selection mechanisms for higher-value segments

archived.title: Apparent obstacles can be reframed as selection mechanisms that improve unit economics
Obstacles reframed as filters can select for more valuable customer segments
Obstacles that filter for ideal customers become moats competitors cannot copy
Obstacles become advantages when they filter for your ideal customer

notes: All 5 polarity-3 candidates were near-duplicates, not real contradictions. The pair members reference each other as SUPPORTS in their evidence arrays — the polarity metric is flagging mutually-supporting clusters as tensions.
Pairs #3 (INS-260329-58E7|INS-260330-4FED) and #5 (INS-260330-3AB4|INS-260404-8050) each had one member already in archive/dedup-260405/. tension-candidates.json is not filtering out archived IDs.

The Costco-membership-filter cluster has at least 10+ active near-duplicates in startups/strategy and startups/competitive-strategy. Only the 4 appearing in the top 5 polarity-3 pairs were archived in this pass; the remaining ~6 are left for a future dedup.

meta_bugs_surfaced.symptom: polarity_score metric flags near-duplicate clusters with mutually-supporting SUPPORTS edges as 'tensions' (polarity-3 is the maximum but corresponds to tightest semantic clustering, not strongest contradiction).
meta_bugs_surfaced.impact: Tension review flow surfaces dedup targets, not real contradictions. The tension action type cannot be reliably exercised from this queue.

meta_bugs_surfaced.symptom: Candidates reference insights that have been archived (status: archived or moved to archive/ directory). Not filtered out on regeneration.
meta_bugs_surfaced.impact: Stale candidates waste review cycles and create ghost pairs (one side missing).
